titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Thomas Caldecot Chubb Papers
titlestmt.author: by Diane Ducharme and Samuel Sullivan

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday April 29, 2020 at 03:09

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Thomas Caldecot Chubb Papers consist almost entirely of manuscripts, typescripts and galley proofs of Chubb's published works, including Aretino, Scourge of Princes ; Letters of Pietro Aretino ; and Dante and His World .

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Acquired by gift and bequest from Thomas Caldecot Chubb, 1940-1974.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Thomas Caldecot Chubb Papers are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Thomas Caldecot Chubb Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The collection is comprised of material formerly classed as: Uncat ZA Chubb, ZA Chubb, and MS Vault Chubb. It received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization, in 2011.

bioghist.head: Thomas Caldecot Chubb (1899-1972)
bioghist.p: Thomas Caldecot Chubb was born in East Orange, New Jersey, the son of insurance exective Herndon Chubb and his wife, Alice Lee Chubb. He was educated at St. Paul's School and at Yale College, receiving his BA in 1922. It was at Yale that he achieved his first literary successes. His poetry collection, The White God and Other Poems , was published in the Yale Younger Poets series in 1920, and his poem Kyrdoon was the Yale University Prize Poem in 1921.

Although he served in the Naval Reserve in World War I and in the Office of Strategic Services in World War II, Chubb was self-employed as a writer for most of his adult life. In addition to several collections of verse, he published biographies of Dante, Boccaccio and Aretino; translations of Aretino's letters and Cecco Angiolieri's sonnets; and histories of the Byzantines, Venetians, and Vikings for younger readers. He also contributed a number of book reviews to the New York Times Sunday Book Review.

Chubb was also a competitive sailor and member of the New York Yacht Club, the Royal Bermuda Yacht Club, and the Indian River Yacht Club. He was a trustee of the Chubb Foundation, the Rosemary Hall Foundation and the Yale Library Associates, as well as a fellow of Timothy Dwight College at Yale. He was active in the Connecticut Democratic Party, a member of the Merritt Parkway Commission, and served on the Connecticut Commission on the Arts.

In his later years, Chubb lived in Greenwich, Connecticut and Springfield Plantation, Thomasville, Georgia, with his third wife, Edith Onions Chubb. He died in Georgia on March 22, 1972, and was survived by his widow, a son, and two daughters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Thomas Caldecot Chubb Papers consist almost entirely of manuscripts, typescripts and galley proofs of Chubb's published works, including his early poetry collection, The White God; his biographies Aretino, Scourge of Princes and Dante and His World; and his histories for young readers, including The Venetians: Merchant Princes and The Byzantines.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series. I. Poetry, 1920-1969. II. Prose, 1929-1970.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains typescripts, galleys, and corrected galleys for several collections of poetry by Chubb.

title.part: The Sonnets of a Handsome and Well-Mannered Rogue

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title of work.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series consists almost entirely of typescripts and galleys of several prose works by Chubb, including his Aretino, Scourge of Princes and Dante and His World .
